title: haplopappus anthylloides
intro: Haplopappus anthylloides, also known as antelope brush, is a shrub-like plant native to the western United States and Canada. It is a member of the sunflower family and has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as sore throats, wounds, and skin irritations, due to its antiseptic and antibacterial properties. Haplopappus anthylloides can be prepared as an herbal infusion, tincture, or salve, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for various health benefits.
